ranking.name: recurrent_dim_scaffolded_readout
ranking.why: shared recurrent scaffold helps the abstract-family readout stay aligned with the object manifold while preserving switching-aware transport.

ranking.name: dual_overlap_transport_operator
ranking.why: memory-assisted transport remains strong but gains slightly less from reasoning-compatible scaffold reuse.

ranking.name: family_low_rank_readout_operator
ranking.why: low-rank alignment helps family geometry but is weaker on cross-phase scaffold stability.

ranking.name: baseline_filtered_readout
ranking.why: baseline remains the control condition for gap-aligned comparison.

verdict.core_answer: Under a benchmark aligned to the main encoding gap plus reasoning and causal-projection constraints, recurrent_dim_scaffolded_readout remains the best current P3 operator.
verdict.next_engineering_target: promote recurrent_dim_scaffolded_readout into the next filtered benchmark loop and keep dual_overlap_transport_operator as the strongest contrast baseline.
